addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

14	
15	[![Build status by AppVeyor](https://ci.appveyor.com/api/projects/status/r5dtv6amsppigpsp/branch/release?svg=true)](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/Paebbels/poc/branch/release)

20	
21	This library is published and maintained by **Chair for VLSI Design, Diagnostics and Architecture** - 
22	Faculty of Computer Science, Technische Universität Dresden, Germany 
23	**http://vlsi-eda.inf.tu-dresden.de**
24

26	
27	Table of Content:
28	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
29	 1. [Overview](#1-overview)
30	 2. [Quick Start Guide](#2-quick-start-guide)  
31	    2.1. [Requirements and Dependencies](#21-requirements-and-dependencies)  
32	    2.2. [Download](#22-download)  
33	    2.3. [Configuring PoC on a Local System](#23-configuring-poc-on-a-local-system)  
34	    2.4. [Integration](#24-integration)  
35	    2.5. [Updating](#25-updating)
36	 3. [Common Notes](#3-common-notes)
37	 4. [Cite the PoC-Library](#4-cite-the-poc-library)
38	
39	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	
41	## 1 Overview
42	
43	PoC - “Pile of Cores” provides implementations for often required hardware functions such as
44	Arithmetic Units, Caches, Clock-Domain-Crossing Circuits, FIFOs, RAM wrappers, and I/O Controllers.
45	The hardware modules are typically provided as VHDL or Verilog source code, so it can be easily
46	re-used in a variety of hardware designs.
47	
48	All hardware modules use a common set of VHDL packages to share new VHDL types, sub-programs and
49	constants. Additionally, a set of simulation helper packages eases the writing of testbenches.
50	Because PoC hosts a huge amount of IP cores, all cores are grouped into sub-namespaces to build a
51	clear hierachy.
52	
53	Various simulation and synthesis tool chains are supported to interoperate with PoC. To generalize
54	all supported free and commercial vendor tool chains, PoC is shipped with a Python based
55	infrastructure to offer a command line based frontend.
56	
57	
58	## 2 Quick Start Guide
59	
60	This **Quick Start Guide** gives a fast and simple introduction into PoC. All topics can be found in
61	the [Using PoC][201] section at [ReadTheDocs.io][202] with much more details and examples.
62	
63	
64	### 2.1 Requirements and Dependencies
65	
66	The PoC-Library comes with some scripts to ease most of the common tasks, like running testbenches or
67	generating IP cores. PoC uses Python 3 as a platform independent scripting environment. All Python
68	scripts are wrapped in Bash or PowerShell scripts, to hide some platform specifics of Darwin, Linux or
69	Windows. See [Requirements][211] for further details.
70	
71	[211]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Requirements.html
72	
73	
74	#### PoC requires:
75	 -  A [supported synthesis tool chain][2111], if you want to synthezise IP cores.
76	 -  A [supported simulator tool chain][2112], if you want to simulate IP cores.
77	 -  The **Python 3** programming language and runtime, if you want to use PoC's infrastructure.
78	 -  A shell to execute shell scripts:
79	    -  **Bash** on Linux and OS X
80	    -  **PowerShell** on Windows
81	
82	[2111]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/WhatIsPoC/SupportedToolChains.html
83	[2112]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/WhatIsPoC/SupportedToolChains.html
84	
85	
86	#### PoC optionally requires:
87	 -  **Git command line** tools or
88	 -  **Git User Interface**, if you want to check out the latest 'master' or 'release' branch.
89	
90	
91	#### PoC depends on third part libraries:
92	 -  [Cocotb][2131]  
93	    A coroutine based cosimulation library for writing VHDL and Verilog testbenches in Python.
94	 -  [OS-VVM][2132]  
95	    Open Source VHDL Verification Methodology.
96	 -  [UVVM][2133]  
97	    Universal VHDL Verification Methodology.
98	 -  [VUnit][2134]  
99	    An unit testing framework for VHDL.
100	  
101	All dependencies are available as GitHub repositories and are linked to PoC as Git submodules into the
102	[`PoCRoot\lib`][205] directory. See [Third Party Libraries][206] for more details on these libraries.
103	
104	[2131]: https://github.com/potentialventures/cocotb
105	[2132]: https://github.com/JimLewis/OSVVM
106	[2133]: https://github.com/UVVM/UVVM_All
107	[2134]: https://github.com/VUnit/vunit
108	
109	[201]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/index.html
110	[202]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/
111	[205]: https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC/tree/release/lib
112	[206]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Miscelaneous/ThirdParty.html
113	
114	
115	### 2.2 Download
116	
117	The PoC-Library can be downloaded as a [zip-file][221] (latest 'release' branch), cloned with `git clone`
118	or embedded with `git submodule add` from GitHub. GitHub offers HTTPS and SSH as transfer protocols. See
119	the [Download][222] page for further details. The installation directory is referred to as `PoCRoot`.
120	
121	Protocol | Git Clone Command
122	-------- | :-----------------------------------------------------------
123	HTTPS    | `git clone --recursive https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC.git PoC`
124	SSH      | `git clone --recursive ssh://git@github.com:VLSI-EDA/PoC.git PoC`
125	
126	[221]: https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC/archive/release.zip
127	[222]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Download.html
128	
129	### 2.3 Configuring PoC on a Local System
130	
131	To explore PoC's full potential, it's required to configure some paths and synthesis or simulation tool
132	chains. The following commands start a guided configuration process. Please follow the instructions on
133	screen. It's possible to relaunch the process at any time, for example to register new tools or to update
134	tool versions. See [Configuration][231] for more details. Run the following command line instructions to
135	configure PoC on your local system:
136	
137	```PowerShell
138	cd PoCRoot
139	.\poc.ps1 configure
140	```
141	
142	Use the keyboard buttons: `Y` to accept, `N` to decline, `P` to skip/pass a step and `Return` to accept
143	a default value displayed in brackets.
144	
145	[231]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/PoCConfiguration.html
146	
147	### 2.4 Integration
148	
149	The PoC-Library is meant to be integrated into other HDL projects. Therefore it's recommended to create
150	a library folder and add the PoC-Library as a Git submodule. After the repository linking is done, some
151	short configuration steps are required to setup paths, tool chains and the target platform. The following
152	command line instructions show a short example on how to integrate PoC.
153	
154	#### a) Adding the Library as a Git submodule
155	
156	The following command line instructions will create the folder `lib\PoC\` and clone the PoC-Library as a
157	[Git submodule][2411] into that folder. `ProjectRoot` is the directory of the hosting Git. A detailed list
158	of steps can be found at [Integration][2412].
159	
160	```powershell
161	cd ProjectRoot
162	mkdir lib | cd
163	git submodule add https://github.com:VLSI-EDA/PoC.git PoC
164	cd PoC
165	git remote rename origin github
166	cd ..\..
167	git add .gitmodules lib\PoC
168	git commit -m "Added new git submodule PoC in 'lib\PoC' (PoC-Library)."
169	```
170	
171	[2411]: http://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Tools-Submodules
172	[2412]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Integration.html
173	
174	#### b) Configuring PoC
175	
176	The PoC-Library should be configured to explore its full potential. See [Configuration][2421] for more
177	details. The following command lines will start the configuration process:
178	
179	```powershell
180	cd ProjectRoot
181	.\lib\PoC\poc.ps1 configure
182	```
183	
184	[2421]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/PoCConfiguration.html
185	
186	#### c) Creating PoC's `my_config.vhdl` and `my_project.vhdl` Files
187	
188	The PoC-Library needs two VHDL files for its configuration. These files are used to determine the most
189	suitable implementation depending on the provided target information. Copy the following two template
190	files into your project's source folder. Rename these files to \*.vhdl and configure the VHDL constants
191	in the files:
192	
193	```powershell
194	cd ProjectRoot
195	cp lib\PoC\src\common\my_config.vhdl.template src\common\my_config.vhdl
196	cp lib\PoC\src\common\my_project.vhdl.template src\common\my_project.vhdl
197	```
198	
199	[my_config.vhdl](https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC/blob/release/src/common/my_config.vhdl.template) defines
200	two global constants, which need to be adjusted:
201	
202	```vhdl
203	constant MY_BOARD            : string := "CHANGE THIS"; -- e.g. Custom, ML505, KC705, Atlys
204	constant MY_DEVICE           : string := "CHANGE THIS"; -- e.g. None, XC5VLX50T-1FF1136, EP2SGX90FF1508C3
205	```
206	
207	[my_project.vhdl](https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC/blob/release/src/common/my_project.vhdl.template) also
208	defines two global constants, which need to be adjusted:
209	
210	```vhdl
211	constant MY_PROJECT_DIR      : string := "CHANGE THIS"; -- e.g. d:/vhdl/myproject/, /home/me/projects/myproject/"
212	constant MY_OPERATING_SYSTEM : string := "CHANGE THIS"; -- e.g. WINDOWS, LINUX
213	```
214	
215	Further informations are provided at [Creating my_config/my_project.vhdl][2431].
216	
217	[2431]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/VHDLConfiguration.html
218	
219	#### d) Adding PoC's Common Packages to a Synthesis or Simulation Project
220	
221	PoC is shipped with a set of common packages, which are used by most of its modules. These packages are
222	stored in the `PoCRoot\src\common` directory. PoC also provides a VHDL context in `common.vhdl` , which
223	can be used to reference all packages at once.
224	
225	
226	#### e) Adding PoC's Simulation Packages to a Simulation Project
227	
228	Simulation projects additionally require PoC's simulation helper packages, which are located in the
229	`PoCRoot\src\sim` directory. Because some VHDL version are incompatible among each other, PoC uses
230	version suffixes like `*.v93.vhdl` or `*.v08.vhdl` in the file name to denote the supported VHDL version
231	of a file.
232	
233	
234	#### f) Compiling Shipped IP Cores
235	
236	Some IP Cores are shipped as pre-configured vendor IP Cores. If such IP cores shall be used in a HDL
237	project, it's recommended to use PoC to create, compile and if needed patch these IP cores. See
238	[Synthesis][2461] for more details.
239	
240	[2461]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Synthesis.html
241	
242	
243	### 2.5 Updating
244	
245	The PoC-Library can be updated by using `git fetch` and `git merge`.
246	
247	```PowerShell
248	cd PoCRoot
249	# update the local repository
250	git fetch --prune
251	# review the commit tree and messages, using the 'treea' alias
252	git treea
253	# if all changes are OK, do a fast-forward merge
254	git merge
255	```
256	
257	**See also:**
258	 -  [**Running one or more testbenches**][251]  
259	    The installation can be checked by running one or more of PoC's testbenches.
260	 -  [**Running one or more netlist generation flows**][252]  
261	    The installation can also be checked by running one or more of PoC's synthesis flows.
262	
263	[251]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Simulation.html
264	[252]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Synthesis.html 
265	
266	
267	## 3. Common Notes
268	
269	**The PoC-Library** is structured into several sub-folders naming the purpose of the folder like
270	[`src`](src) for sources files or [`tb`](tb) for testbench files. The structure within these folders
271	is always the same and based on PoC's sub-namespace tree.
272	
273	**Main directory overview:**
274	
275	 -  [`lib`](lib) - Embedded or linked external libraries.
276	 -  [`netlist`](netlist) - Configuration files and output directory for pre-configured netlist synthesis
277	    results from vendor IP cores or from complex PoC controllers.
278	 -  [`py`](py) - Supporting Python scripts.
279	 -  [`sim`](sim) - Pre-configured waveform views for selected testbenches.
280	 -  [`src`](src) - PoC's source files grouped into sub-folders according to the sub-namespace tree.
281	 -  [`tb`](tb) - Testbench files.
282	 -  [`tcl`](tcl) - Tcl files.
283	 -  [`temp`](temp) - Automatically created temporary directors for various tools used by PoC's Python scripts.
284	 -  [`tools`](tools) - Settings/highlighting files and helpers for supported tools.
285	 -  [`ucf`](ucf) - Pre-configured constraint files (\*.ucf, \*.xdc, \*.sdc) for supported FPGA boards.
286	 -  [`xst`](xst) - Configuration files to synthesize PoC modules with Xilinx XST into a netlist.
287	
288	
289	All VHDL source files should be compiled into the VHDL library `PoC`. If not indicated otherwise, all
290	source files can be compiled using the VHDL-93 or VHDL-2008 language version. Incompatible files are
291	named `*.v93.vhdl` and `*.v08.vhdl` to denote the highest supported language version.
292	
293	
294	## 4 Cite the PoC-Library
295	
296	If you are using the PoC-Library, please let us know. We are grateful for your project's reference.
297	The PoC-Library hosted at [GitHub.com](https://www.github.com). Please use the following
298	[biblatex](https://www.ctan.org/pkg/biblatex) entry to cite us:
299	
300	```bibtex
301	# BibLaTex example entry
302	@online{poc,
303	  title={{PoC - Pile of Cores}},
304	  author={{Chair of VLSI Design, Diagnostics and Architecture}},
305	  organization={{Technische Universität Dresden}},
306	  year={2016},
307	  url={https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC},
308	  urldate={2016-10-28},
309	}
310	```
311	
312	
313	## License
314	[![FOSSA Status](https://app.fossa.io/api/projects/git%2Bgithub.com%2FVLSI-EDA%2FPoC.svg?type=large)](https://app.fossa.io/projects/git%2Bgithub.com%2FVLSI-EDA%2FPoC?ref=badge_large)

# The PoC-Library

[![Build status by AppVeyor](https://ci.appveyor.com/api/projects/status/r5dtv6amsppigpsp/branch/release?svg=true)](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/Paebbels/poc/branch/release)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/poc-library/badge/?version=latest)](http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)
[![FOSSA Status](https://app.fossa.io/api/projects/git%2Bgithub.com%2FVLSI-EDA%2FPoC.svg?type=shield)](https://app.fossa.io/projects/git%2Bgithub.com%2FVLSI-EDA%2FPoC?ref=badge_shield)  
![Latest tag](https://img.shields.io/github/tag/VLSI-EDA/PoC.svg?style=flat)[![Latest release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/VLSI-EDA/PoC.svg?style=flat)](https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC/releases)[![Apache License 2.0](https://img.shields.io/github/license/VLSI-EDA/PoC.svg?style=flat)](LICENSE.md)


This library is published and maintained by **Chair for VLSI Design, Diagnostics and Architecture** - 
Faculty of Computer Science, Technische Universität Dresden, Germany 
**http://vlsi-eda.inf.tu-dresden.de**

## 1 Overview

PoC - “Pile of Cores” provides implementations for often required hardware functions such as
Arithmetic Units, Caches, Clock-Domain-Crossing Circuits, FIFOs, RAM wrappers, and I/O Controllers.
The hardware modules are typically provided as VHDL or Verilog source code, so it can be easily
re-used in a variety of hardware designs.

All hardware modules use a common set of VHDL packages to share new VHDL types, sub-programs and
constants. Additionally, a set of simulation helper packages eases the writing of testbenches.
Because PoC hosts a huge amount of IP cores, all cores are grouped into sub-namespaces to build a
clear hierachy.

Various simulation and synthesis tool chains are supported to interoperate with PoC. To generalize
all supported free and commercial vendor tool chains, PoC is shipped with a Python based
infrastructure to offer a command line based frontend.


## 2 Quick Start Guide

This **Quick Start Guide** gives a fast and simple introduction into PoC. All topics can be found in
the [Using PoC][201] section at [ReadTheDocs.io][202] with much more details and examples.


### 2.1 Requirements and Dependencies

The PoC-Library comes with some scripts to ease most of the common tasks, like running testbenches or
generating IP cores. PoC uses Python 3 as a platform independent scripting environment. All Python
scripts are wrapped in Bash or PowerShell scripts, to hide some platform specifics of Darwin, Linux or
Windows. See [Requirements][211] for further details.

[211]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Requirements.html


#### PoC requires:
 -  A [supported synthesis tool chain][2111], if you want to synthezise IP cores.
 -  A [supported simulator tool chain][2112], if you want to simulate IP cores.
 -  The **Python 3** programming language and runtime, if you want to use PoC's infrastructure.
 -  A shell to execute shell scripts:
    -  **Bash** on Linux and OS X
    -  **PowerShell** on Windows

[2111]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/WhatIsPoC/SupportedToolChains.html
[2112]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/WhatIsPoC/SupportedToolChains.html


#### PoC optionally requires:
 -  **Git command line** tools or
 -  **Git User Interface**, if you want to check out the latest 'master' or 'release' branch.


#### PoC depends on third part libraries:
 -  [Cocotb][2131]  
    A coroutine based cosimulation library for writing VHDL and Verilog testbenches in Python.
 -  [OS-VVM][2132]  
    Open Source VHDL Verification Methodology.
 -  [UVVM][2133]  
    Universal VHDL Verification Methodology.
 -  [VUnit][2134]  
    An unit testing framework for VHDL.
  
All dependencies are available as GitHub repositories and are linked to PoC as Git submodules into the
[`PoCRoot\lib`][205] directory. See [Third Party Libraries][206] for more details on these libraries.

[2131]: https://github.com/potentialventures/cocotb
[2132]: https://github.com/JimLewis/OSVVM
[2133]: https://github.com/UVVM/UVVM_All
[2134]: https://github.com/VUnit/vunit

[201]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/index.html
[202]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/
[205]: https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC/tree/release/lib
[206]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Miscelaneous/ThirdParty.html


### 2.2 Download

The PoC-Library can be downloaded as a [zip-file][221] (latest 'release' branch), cloned with `git clone`
or embedded with `git submodule add` from GitHub. GitHub offers HTTPS and SSH as transfer protocols. See
the [Download][222] page for further details. The installation directory is referred to as `PoCRoot`.

Protocol | Git Clone Command
-------- | :-----------------------------------------------------------
HTTPS    | `git clone --recursive https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC.git PoC`
SSH      | `git clone --recursive ssh://git@github.com:VLSI-EDA/PoC.git PoC`

[221]: https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC/archive/release.zip
[222]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Download.html

### 2.3 Configuring PoC on a Local System

To explore PoC's full potential, it's required to configure some paths and synthesis or simulation tool
chains. The following commands start a guided configuration process. Please follow the instructions on
screen. It's possible to relaunch the process at any time, for example to register new tools or to update
tool versions. See [Configuration][231] for more details. Run the following command line instructions to
configure PoC on your local system:

```PowerShell
cd PoCRoot
.\poc.ps1 configure
```

Use the keyboard buttons: `Y` to accept, `N` to decline, `P` to skip/pass a step and `Return` to accept
a default value displayed in brackets.

[231]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/PoCConfiguration.html

### 2.4 Integration

The PoC-Library is meant to be integrated into other HDL projects. Therefore it's recommended to create
a library folder and add the PoC-Library as a Git submodule. After the repository linking is done, some
short configuration steps are required to setup paths, tool chains and the target platform. The following
command line instructions show a short example on how to integrate PoC.

#### a) Adding the Library as a Git submodule

The following command line instructions will create the folder `lib\PoC\` and clone the PoC-Library as a
[Git submodule][2411] into that folder. `ProjectRoot` is the directory of the hosting Git. A detailed list
of steps can be found at [Integration][2412].

```powershell
cd ProjectRoot
mkdir lib | cd
git submodule add https://github.com:VLSI-EDA/PoC.git PoC
cd PoC
git remote rename origin github
cd ..\..
git add .gitmodules lib\PoC
git commit -m "Added new git submodule PoC in 'lib\PoC' (PoC-Library)."
```

[2411]: http://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Tools-Submodules
[2412]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Integration.html

#### b) Configuring PoC

The PoC-Library should be configured to explore its full potential. See [Configuration][2421] for more
details. The following command lines will start the configuration process:

```powershell
cd ProjectRoot
.\lib\PoC\poc.ps1 configure
```

[2421]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/PoCConfiguration.html

#### c) Creating PoC's `my_config.vhdl` and `my_project.vhdl` Files

The PoC-Library needs two VHDL files for its configuration. These files are used to determine the most
suitable implementation depending on the provided target information. Copy the following two template
files into your project's source folder. Rename these files to \*.vhdl and configure the VHDL constants
in the files:

```powershell
cd ProjectRoot
cp lib\PoC\src\common\my_config.vhdl.template src\common\my_config.vhdl
cp lib\PoC\src\common\my_project.vhdl.template src\common\my_project.vhdl
```

[my_config.vhdl](https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC/blob/release/src/common/my_config.vhdl.template) defines
two global constants, which need to be adjusted:

```vhdl
constant MY_BOARD            : string := "CHANGE THIS"; -- e.g. Custom, ML505, KC705, Atlys
constant MY_DEVICE           : string := "CHANGE THIS"; -- e.g. None, XC5VLX50T-1FF1136, EP2SGX90FF1508C3
```

[my_project.vhdl](https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC/blob/release/src/common/my_project.vhdl.template) also
defines two global constants, which need to be adjusted:

```vhdl
constant MY_PROJECT_DIR      : string := "CHANGE THIS"; -- e.g. d:/vhdl/myproject/, /home/me/projects/myproject/"
constant MY_OPERATING_SYSTEM : string := "CHANGE THIS"; -- e.g. WINDOWS, LINUX
```

Further informations are provided at [Creating my_config/my_project.vhdl][2431].

[2431]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/VHDLConfiguration.html

#### d) Adding PoC's Common Packages to a Synthesis or Simulation Project

PoC is shipped with a set of common packages, which are used by most of its modules. These packages are
stored in the `PoCRoot\src\common` directory. PoC also provides a VHDL context in `common.vhdl` , which
can be used to reference all packages at once.


#### e) Adding PoC's Simulation Packages to a Simulation Project

Simulation projects additionally require PoC's simulation helper packages, which are located in the
`PoCRoot\src\sim` directory. Because some VHDL version are incompatible among each other, PoC uses
version suffixes like `*.v93.vhdl` or `*.v08.vhdl` in the file name to denote the supported VHDL version
of a file.


#### f) Compiling Shipped IP Cores

Some IP Cores are shipped as pre-configured vendor IP Cores. If such IP cores shall be used in a HDL
project, it's recommended to use PoC to create, compile and if needed patch these IP cores. See
[Synthesis][2461] for more details.

[2461]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Synthesis.html


### 2.5 Updating

The PoC-Library can be updated by using `git fetch` and `git merge`.

```PowerShell
cd PoCRoot
# update the local repository
git fetch --prune
# review the commit tree and messages, using the 'treea' alias
git treea
# if all changes are OK, do a fast-forward merge
git merge
```

**See also:**
 -  [**Running one or more testbenches**][251]  
    The installation can be checked by running one or more of PoC's testbenches.
 -  [**Running one or more netlist generation flows**][252]  
    The installation can also be checked by running one or more of PoC's synthesis flows.

[251]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Simulation.html
[252]: http://poc-library.readthedocs.io/en/latest/UsingPoC/Synthesis.html 


## 3. Common Notes

**The PoC-Library** is structured into several sub-folders naming the purpose of the folder like
[`src`](src) for sources files or [`tb`](tb) for testbench files. The structure within these folders
is always the same and based on PoC's sub-namespace tree.

**Main directory overview:**

 -  [`lib`](lib) - Embedded or linked external libraries.
 -  [`netlist`](netlist) - Configuration files and output directory for pre-configured netlist synthesis
    results from vendor IP cores or from complex PoC controllers.
 -  [`py`](py) - Supporting Python scripts.
 -  [`sim`](sim) - Pre-configured waveform views for selected testbenches.
 -  [`src`](src) - PoC's source files grouped into sub-folders according to the sub-namespace tree.
 -  [`tb`](tb) - Testbench files.
 -  [`tcl`](tcl) - Tcl files.
 -  [`temp`](temp) - Automatically created temporary directors for various tools used by PoC's Python scripts.
 -  [`tools`](tools) - Settings/highlighting files and helpers for supported tools.
 -  [`ucf`](ucf) - Pre-configured constraint files (\*.ucf, \*.xdc, \*.sdc) for supported FPGA boards.
 -  [`xst`](xst) - Configuration files to synthesize PoC modules with Xilinx XST into a netlist.


All VHDL source files should be compiled into the VHDL library `PoC`. If not indicated otherwise, all
source files can be compiled using the VHDL-93 or VHDL-2008 language version. Incompatible files are
named `*.v93.vhdl` and `*.v08.vhdl` to denote the highest supported language version.


## 4 Cite the PoC-Library

If you are using the PoC-Library, please let us know. We are grateful for your project's reference.
The PoC-Library hosted at [GitHub.com](https://www.github.com). Please use the following
[biblatex](https://www.ctan.org/pkg/biblatex) entry to cite us:

```bibtex
# BibLaTex example entry
@online{poc,
  title={{PoC - Pile of Cores}},
  author={{Chair of VLSI Design, Diagnostics and Architecture}},
  organization={{Technische Universität Dresden}},
  year={2016},
  url={https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC},
  urldate={2016-10-28},
}
```

#! /usr/bin/env bash
# EMACS settings: -*-	tab-width: 2; indent-tabs-mode: t -*-
# vim: tabstop=2:shiftwidth=2:noexpandtab
# kate: tab-width 2; replace-tabs off; indent-width 2;

#	Authors:				 	Patrick Lehmann
#                   Martin Zabel
#
#	Bash Script:			Wrapper Script to execute <PoC-Root>/py/PoC.py
#
# Description:
# ------------------------------------
#	This is a bash wrapper script (executable) which:
#		- saves the current working directory as an environment variable
#		- delegates the call to <PoC-Root>/py/wrapper.sh
#

# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================
#
# Change this, if pyIPCMI solutions and pyIPCMI projects are used
Library_RelPath="."  # relative path to PoC root directory
Library="PoC"        # library name
Solution=""          # solution name
Project=""           # project name

# Configure pyIPCMI environment here
pyIPCMI_Dir="lib/pyIPCMI"
pyIPCMI_BashModule="pyIPCMI"

# work around for Darwin (Mac OS)
READLINK=readlink; if [[ $(uname) == "Darwin" ]]; then READLINK=greadlink; fi

# resolve script directory
# solution is from http://stackoverflow.com/questions/59895/can-a-bash-script-tell-what-directory-its-stored-in
SOURCE="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
while [ -h "$SOURCE" ]; do													# resolve $SOURCE until the file is no longer a symlink
  DIR="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$SOURCE" )" && pwd )"
  SOURCE="$($READLINK "$SOURCE")"
  [[ $SOURCE != /* ]] && SOURCE="$DIR/$SOURCE"			# if $SOURCE was a relative symlink, we need to resolve it relative to the path where the symlink file was located
done
SCRIPT_DIR="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$SOURCE" )" && pwd )"

# save parameters and script directory
Wrapper_Parameters=$@
Wrapper_WorkingDirectory=$(pwd)
Library_RootDir_RelPath="$SCRIPT_DIR/."
Library_RootDirectory=$(cd "$Library_RootDir_RelPath/$Library_RelPath" && pwd)

# invoke main wrapper
source "$Library_RootDirectory/$pyIPCMI_Dir/$pyIPCMI_BashModule.sh"

# return exit status
exit $PoC_ExitCode

1	-- EMACS settings: -*-  tab-width: 2; indent-tabs-mode: t -*-
2	-- vim: tabstop=2:shiftwidth=2:noexpandtab
3	-- kate: tab-width 2; replace-tabs off; indent-width 2;
4	-- =============================================================================
5	-- Authors:					Thomas B. Preusser
6	--									Steffen Koehler
7	--									Martin Zabel
8	--									Patrick Lehmann
9	--
10	-- Entity:					FIFO, Common Clock (cc), Pipelined Interface
11	--
12	-- Description:
13	-- -------------------------------------
14	-- This module implements a regular FIFO with common clock (cc), pipelined
15	-- interface. Common clock means read and write port use the same clock. The
16	-- FIFO size can be configured in word width (``D_BITS``) and minimum word count
17	-- ``MIN_DEPTH``. The specified depth is rounded up to the next suitable value.
18	--
19	-- ``DATA_REG`` (=true) is a hint, that distributed memory or registers should
20	-- be used as data storage. The actual memory type depends on the device
21	-- architecture. See implementation for details.
22	--
23	-- ``*STATE_*_BITS`` defines the granularity of the fill state indicator
24	-- ``*state_*``. If a fill state is not of interest, set ``*STATE_*_BITS = 0``.
25	-- ``fstate_rd`` is associated with the read clock domain and outputs the
26	-- guaranteed number of words available in the FIFO. ``estate_wr`` is associated
27	-- with the write clock domain and outputs the number of words that is
28	-- guaranteed to be accepted by the FIFO without a capacity overflow. Note that
29	-- both these indicators cannot replace the ``full`` or ``valid`` outputs as
30	-- they may be implemented as giving pessimistic bounds that are minimally off
31	-- the true fill state.
32	--
33	-- ``fstate_rd`` and ``estate_wr`` are combinatorial outputs and include an address
34	-- comparator (subtractor) in their path.
35	--
36	-- .. rubric:: Examples:
37	--
38	-- * FSTATE_RD_BITS = 1:
39	--
40	--   +-----------+----------------------+
41	--   | fstate_rd | filled (at least)    |
42	--   +===========+======================+
43	--   |    0      | 0/2 full             |
44	--   +-----------+----------------------+
45	--   |    1      | 1/2 full (half full) |
46	--   +-----------+----------------------+
47	--
48	-- * FSTATE_RD_BITS = 2:
49	--
50	--   +-----------+----------------------+
51	--   | fstate_rd | filled (at least)    |
52	--   +===========+======================+
53	--   |    0      | 0/4 full             |
54	--   +-----------+----------------------+
55	--   |    1      | 1/4 full             |
56	--   +-----------+----------------------+
57	--   |    2      | 2/4 full (half full) |
58	--   +-----------+----------------------+
59	--   |    3      | 3/4 full             |
60	--   +-----------+----------------------+
61	--
62	-- SeeAlso:
63	-- :ref:`IP:fifo_dc_got`
64	--   For a FIFO with dependent clocks.
65	-- :ref:`IP:fifo_ic_got`
66	--   For a FIFO with independent clocks (cross-clock FIFO).
67	-- :ref:`IP:fifo_glue`
68	--   For a minimal FIFO / pipeline decoupling.
69	--

--									Steffen Koehler
--									Martin Zabel
--									Patrick Lehmann
--
-- Entity:					FIFO, Common Clock (cc), Pipelined Interface
--
-- Description:
-- -------------------------------------
-- This module implements a regular FIFO with common clock (cc), pipelined
-- interface. Common clock means read and write port use the same clock. The
-- FIFO size can be configured in word width (``D_BITS``) and minimum word count
-- ``MIN_DEPTH``. The specified depth is rounded up to the next suitable value.
--
-- ``DATA_REG`` (=true) is a hint, that distributed memory or registers should
-- be used as data storage. The actual memory type depends on the device
-- architecture. See implementation for details.
--
-- ``*STATE_*_BITS`` defines the granularity of the fill state indicator
-- ``*state_*``. If a fill state is not of interest, set ``*STATE_*_BITS = 0``.
-- ``fstate_rd`` is associated with the read clock domain and outputs the
-- guaranteed number of words available in the FIFO. ``estate_wr`` is associated
-- with the write clock domain and outputs the number of words that is
-- guaranteed to be accepted by the FIFO without a capacity overflow. Note that
-- both these indicators cannot replace the ``full`` or ``valid`` outputs as
-- they may be implemented as giving pessimistic bounds that are minimally off
-- the true fill state.
--
-- ``fstate_rd`` and ``estate_wr`` are combinatorial outputs and include an address
-- comparator (subtractor) in their path.
--
-- .. rubric:: Examples:
--
-- * FSTATE_RD_BITS = 1:
--
--   +-----------+----------------------+
--   | fstate_rd | filled (at least)    |
--   +===========+======================+
--   |    0      | 0/2 full             |
--   +-----------+----------------------+
--   |    1      | 1/2 full (half full) |
--   +-----------+----------------------+
--
-- * FSTATE_RD_BITS = 2:
--
--   +-----------+----------------------+
--   | fstate_rd | filled (at least)    |
--   +===========+======================+
--   |    0      | 0/4 full             |
--   +-----------+----------------------+
--   |    1      | 1/4 full             |
--   +-----------+----------------------+
--   |    2      | 2/4 full (half full) |
--   +-----------+----------------------+
--   |    3      | 3/4 full             |
--   +-----------+----------------------+
--
-- SeeAlso:
-- :ref:`IP:fifo_dc_got`
--   For a FIFO with dependent clocks.
-- :ref:`IP:fifo_ic_got`
--   For a FIFO with independent clocks (cross-clock FIFO).
-- :ref:`IP:fifo_glue`
--   For a minimal FIFO / pipeline decoupling.

# PoC VHDL Coding Guide

## Licensing
PoC is published under the [Apache License, Version 2.0](LICENSE.md).
Please, make sure you are able and willing to submit your contributions to
this license.

## Naming
1. VHDL sources have the file extension `.vhdl`.
2. Prepend the name of an entity with its containing package using snake case,
   e.g.: `arith_addw` for the wide adder in package `arith`. Each module is
   implemented in its own source file, the name of which is `<entity>.vhdl`.
3. Synthesizable module implementations are provided through an
   architecture named `rtl`.
4. The name of a testbench entity copies the name of the tested module or
   package, to which `_tb` is appended. Its implementing architecture is
   named `tb`.

## Formatting

### Header
* Include the configuration header to automatically configure common editors
  to the tab width of two spaces:
```vhdl
-- EMACS settings: -*-  tab-width: 2; indent-tabs-mode: t -*-
-- vim: tabstop=2:shiftwidth=2:noexpandtab
-- kate: tab-width 2; replace-tabs off; indent-width 2;
```
* Document your package or module within a documentation header:
 * starting with a separator line matching `/^--\s*={16,}$/`,
 * containing the appropriate sections of
   `Authors|Entity|Description|SeeAlso`, and
 * providing the license statement:
```vhdl
-- ===========================================================================
-- Copyright 2007-2016 Technische Universitaet Dresden - Germany
-- 	     	             Chair for VLSI-Design, Diagnostics and Architecture
--
-- Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
-- you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
-- You may obtain a copy of the License at
--
--              http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
--
-- Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
-- distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
-- WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
-- See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
-- limitations under the License.
-- ===========================================================================
```
  
### Whitespace
* Indent with one tab character per indentation level.
* Assume a tab width of two spaces.
* Eliminate all trailing whitespace.

## Coding Style

### Capitalization
1. Both snake and camel case are acceptable for signal and variable names as
   well as for labels. Be consistent in the capitalization when naming any such
   instance.
2. Use all lower case for VHDL keywords (e.g. `process`, `case`, ...)
   and common standard types (e.g. `integer`, `std_logic_vector`).
3. Use all upper case for constants and generic parameters.

### Signal Initialization
1. Specify an initializer for all signals that are to represent sequential
   logic, i.e. some state. If their initial state is irrelevant, initialize
   them to a don't-care value as appropriate, e.g. `(others => '-')`. Typically
   the same initial state should be assigned upon a reset condition.
2. Combinational signals never have a user-specified initializer.

### Expressions
1. Always use parentheses within expressions as soon as operator precedence
   is relevant and not trivial.
2. Avoid extraneous parentheses around expressions already framed by their
   syntactical contexts, such as `if ... then` or `while ... loop`.

I now have a comprehensive understanding of the entire PoC-Library project. Let me consolidate my understanding:

## Project: PoC (Pile of Cores)
- A VHDL/Verilog IP core library for FPGA/ASIC hardware design
- Published by TU Dresden, Chair for VLSI Design
- Note: Forked to github.com/VHDL/PoC (this is the archived original)

## Architecture / Key Patterns:

1. **Namespace organization**: All modules grouped into sub-namespaces (src/arith, src/bus, src/cache, src/comm, src/common, src/dstruct, src/fifo, src/io, src/mem, src/misc, src/net, src/sim, src/sort, src/xil, src/alt)
   - Each namespace has a `<ns>.pkg.vhdl` (e.g., arith.pkg.vhdl, fifo.pkg.vhdl) with component declarations, types, functions
   - Each module is one entity in its own file `<entity>.vhdl` with `rtl` architecture
   - Testbench = `<entity>_tb.vhdl` with `tb` architecture

2. **Configuration mechanism** (central to PoC portability):
   - `my_config.vhdl` (from template) - sets MY_BOARD, MY_DEVICE
   - `my_project.vhdl` - sets MY_PROJECT_DIR, MY_OPERATING_SYSTEM
   - `src/common/config.vhdl` - evaluates my_config, builds T_BOARD_INFO, T_DEVICE_INFO, DEVICE_INFO constant
   - VENDOR_ALTERA, VENDOR_XILINX, VENDOR_LATTICE, VENDOR_GENERIC enums

3. **Vendor selection mechanism** (the key portability feature):
   - A portable entity (e.g., sync_Bits, ocram_sp) reads DEV_INFO / DEVICE_INFO
   - Uses `generate` statements to instantiate vendor-specific primitives (sync_Bits_Xilinx, sync_Bits_Altera, ocram_sp_altera)
   - Generic fallback when no vendor recognized

4. **Common packages** (src/common): utils, config, math, strings, vectors, physical, components, fileio (v08/v93), debug, common.vhdl (context)
   - physical.vhdl defines FREQ type (used as `100 MHz` in testbenches)
   - components.vhdl - flip-flops/mux as functions (ffdre, etc.)

5. **Compile order** managed via `*.files` files (common.files, sim.files, namespace .files) consumed by pyIPCMI infrastructure

6. **VHDL version handling**: `.v93.vhdl` and `.v08.vhdl` suffixes for version-specific files (protected types, file IO)

7. **Infrastructure**: 
   - `poc.sh` / `poc.ps1` - entry wrappers
   - delegate to `lib/pyIPCMI` (Python-based, git submodule) - command line frontend for simulation/synthesis
   - `*.files` files define compile lists
   - netlist/, xst/ - out-of-context synthesis
   - ucf/ - FPGA board constraints

8. **Simulation helpers** (src/sim): sim_types, sim_global, sim_simulation (simInitialize, simGenerateClock, simGenerateWaveform), sim_random, sim_waveform, sim_VCDParser

9. **Testbenches** (tb/): mirror src/ namespace structure, use sim packages, board-specific my_config variants (my_config_GENERIC, my_config_KC705, etc.)

10. **Third-party libs** (lib/): cocotb, osvvm, uvvm, vunit (as git submodules)

input.head: 8c39b2407a97ec81b8601c4b64e4d2e2141ab9cf
input.rationale: PoC（Pile of Cores）是一个大型 VHDL/Verilog IP 核库，学习的关键不在于逐个记忆数百个核，而在于掌握贯穿全库的少数核心机制：命名空间分包模式、my_config/DEVICE_INFO 配置机制、基于 generate 的厂商可移植选择、公共包体系、仿真辅助包，以及 pyIPCMI 命令行基础设施。因此大纲分三层五单元：第 1 单元（入门）让读者从零认识项目定位、目录结构、运行配置与编码规范；第 2 单元（进阶）拆解 src/common 公共包与配置机制，这是理解一切后续核的基础；第 3 单元（进阶）讲解 IP 核的通用编写与组织模式，重点落在厂商选择、ocram/FIFO/arith/sync/io 等代表性家族；第 4 单元（进阶）覆盖仿真辅助包、测试台写法、VHDL 版本处理、综合流程与板级约束；第 5 单元（专家）深入 pyIPCMI 基础设施、第三方验证库集成，以及 cache/bus/net 复杂子系统，最后落到如何扩展 PoC。依赖关系呈清晰的有向无环图，每个讲义只解决一个主题并绑定真实源码与一个动手任务。

lectures.title: PoC 项目总览与定位
lectures.topic: 介绍 PoC（Pile of Cores）是什么、解决什么问题、由谁维护，以及它已迁移到 VHDL/PoC 仓库的现状。
learning_goals: 理解 PoC 是一个可复用的 VHDL/Verilog 硬件 IP 核库
知道 PoC 提供的核心能力（算术、缓存、CDC、FIFO、RAM、IO 控制器等）
了解项目的维护方与迁移到 VHDL/PoC 的现状
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README 的 Overview 部分，用自己的话写出 PoC 提供的三类硬件功能，并指出当前仓库与 VHDL/PoC 的关系。

lectures.title: 仓库目录结构解析
lectures.topic: 逐个解释 src、tb、lib、netlist、ucf、xst、py、sim、temp、tools 等顶层目录的用途，建立空间地图。
learning_goals: 记住 PoC 根目录的主要子目录及其职责
理解 src 按子命名空间分组、tb 镜像 src 的组织方式
区分源码、测试台、第三方库、约束、综合输出的位置
lectures.practice_task: 在本地克隆后画出 src/ 下各命名空间目录的树状图，并标注每个目录代表的功能类别（如 arith=算术、fifo=先入先出）。

lectures.title: 获取、运行与配置 PoC
lectures.topic: 讲解如何下载、用 poc.sh/poc.ps1 启动配置流程，以及 my_config.vhdl / my_project.vhdl 两个模板的作用。
learning_goals: 掌握 git clone --recursive 获取含子模块的 PoC
理解 poc.sh / poc.ps1 是调用 Python 基础设施的入口包装脚本
知道 my_config.vhdl 与 my_project.vhdl 的用途及如何从模板创建
lectures.practice_task: 从模板复制并填写 my_config.vhdl，把 MY_BOARD 设为 Custom、MY_DEVICE 设为 None，并说明这两个常量后续会被谁读取。

lectures.title: VHDL 编码规范与命名约定
lectures.topic: 通过 vhdl_coding.md 讲解 PoC 的命名、文件后缀、rtl/tb 架构名、文档头与代码风格约定。
learning_goals: 掌握 <namespace>_<entity> 的蛇形命名与单实体单文件约定
理解综合实现用 rtl 架构、测试台用 _tb 后缀和 tb 架构
了解文档头、缩进与大小写规范
lectures.practice_task: 参照 Entity.template 与 arith_addw.vhdl，为一个虚拟核 mylib_echo 写出符合规范的文件头、entity 声明与 rtl 架构骨架。

units.title: 进阶：公共包与配置机制

lectures.title: 公共包总览与 Common 上下文
lectures.topic: 介绍 src/common 下各公共包的职责，以及 common.vhdl 上下文如何一次性引用它们，并讲解 .files 编译顺序文件。
learning_goals: 了解 utils/config/math/strings/vectors/physical/components 等公共包的作用
理解 VHDL context Common 的批量引用机制
掌握 .files 文件如何描述带条件的编译顺序
lectures.practice_task: 在 common.files 中找到按条件包含 fileio 包的分支，解释为何不同 VHDL 版本会编译不同文件。

lectures.title: utils 包：通用类型与辅助函数
lectures.topic: 深入 utils.vhdl，讲解 T_BOOLVEC/T_INTVEC 等向量类型、整数子类型、枚举类型以及 ite/imin/imax/log2ceil 等常用函数。
learning_goals: 掌握 PoC 自定义的数组和子类型定义
学会使用 ite、imin、imax、log2ceil 等辅助函数
理解 SIMULATION 延迟常量的用途
lectures.practice_task: 在 utils.vhdl 中找到 log2ceil 的定义，写一段测试用例说明它如何把深度 30 映射到地址位宽 5。

lectures.title: 配置机制：my_config 与 config 包
lectures.topic: 讲解 my_config.vhdl 中的 MY_BOARD/MY_DEVICE 如何被 config.vhdl 解析，最终生成 DEVICE_INFO 与 VENDOR 枚举（VENDOR_ALTERA/XILINX/LATTICE 等）。
learning_goals: 理解 my_config 到 DEVICE_INFO 的解析链路
掌握 T_BOARD_INFO、T_DEVICE_INFO 与厂商枚举的作用
明白为什么后续核能根据厂商自动选择实现
lectures.practice_task: 把 MY_DEVICE 改成一个 Xilinx 型号，在 config.vhdl 中追踪它如何被识别为 VENDOR_XILINX，并描述 DEVICE_INFO 记录里包含哪些字段。

lectures.title: physical / strings / vectors / math 包
lectures.topic: 讲解物理类型包（如 FREQ，可在测试台写 100 MHz）、字符串处理、向量类型与数学扩展函数。
learning_goals: 掌握 FREQ/TIME 等物理类型及其字面量写法
了解字符串与向量（如 T_SLM）辅助函数
理解 math 包提供的扩展数学运算
lectures.practice_task: 利用 physical.vhdl 的 FREQ 类型，写出表示 50 MHz 与 10 ns 的常量，并计算二者一个周期的关系。

lectures.title: components 包：用函数描述原语
lectures.topic: 讲解 components.vhdl 如何把触发器（ffdre/ffse）、多路选择器等常用原语以函数形式提供，便于一行调用。
learning_goals: 理解把触发器写成函数的设计思路
掌握 ffdre/ffse/rs 触发器等函数的参数
了解 INIT 参数表示复位值而非上电初值的注意事项
lectures.practice_task: 用 components.vhdl 中的 ffdre 函数实现一个带使能与复位的单比特寄存器进程，并说明 INIT 的真实含义。

units.title: 进阶：IP 核模式与命名空间

lectures.title: 命名空间包模式
lectures.topic: 讲解每个命名空间的 <ns>.pkg.vhdl 如何集中声明组件、类型与函数（以 arith.pkg、fifo.pkg、mem.pkg 为例）。
learning_goals: 掌握命名空间包作为组件声明清单的作用
理解 generic/port 在包中集中声明的复用价值
能够阅读一个新命名空间的 pkg 文件快速了解其核
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 fifo.pkg.vhdl，列出它声明的全部 FIFO 组件名，并标注每个组件的使用场景（如 cc=同钟、ic=跨钟）。

lectures.title: 厂商选择与可移植机制
lectures.topic: 讲解 PoC 如何用 generate 语句依据 DEVICE_INFO.Vendor 在通用实现与厂商专用实现（如 sync_Bits_Xilinx / _Altera）间选择。
learning_goals: 理解可移植实体 + 厂商专用子实体的分层结构
掌握 if generate 选择 VENDOR_ALTERA/XILINX 的写法
了解 ASYNC_REG/SHREG_EXTRACT 等综合属性的作用
lectures.practice_task: 在 sync_Bits.vhdl 中定位 vendor 选择逻辑，画出让一个 Generic 设备走入哪条分支的流程图。

lectures.title: 片上 RAM 抽象：ocram 家族
lectures.topic: 讲解 ocram（on-chip RAM）命名空间如何抽象单端口、简单双端口、真双端口等存储器并对接厂商原语。
learning_goals: 区分 ocram_sp/sdp/tdp/esdp/wf 等存储器配置
理解 ocram 如何根据厂商实例化 altera 版本
掌握读优先/写优先等存储行为差异
lectures.practice_task: 比较 ocram_sp.vhdl 的通用实现与 ocram_sp_altera.vhdl，说明 Altera 分支调用了哪个底层原语。

lectures.title: FIFO 家族
lectures.topic: 讲解 PoC 的 FIFO 矩阵：fifo_glue/fifo_shift/fifo_cc_got/fifo_ic_got 等覆盖同钟、跨钟、暂存回滚等场景。
learning_goals: 区分 cc（同钟）与 ic（独立钟）FIFO
理解 put/got/valid/full 流水线接口语义
掌握 DATA_REG/OUTPUT_REG/STATE_BITS 等关键 generic
lectures.practice_task: 实例化一个 fifo_cc_got，配置 D_BITS=8、MIN_DEPTH=30，并解释 estate_wr/fstate_rd 填充指示器的含义。

lectures.title: 算术单元：arith 命名空间
lectures.topic: 讲解 arith 命名空间下的计数器、宽加法、伪随机数、优先编码、开方与除法等算术核。
learning_goals: 掌握 free/gray/bcd/ring 等各类计数器
理解 arith_firstone 优先编码与令牌传递
了解 arith_prng 伪随机与 arith_sqrt 除法实现
lectures.practice_task: 实例化 arith_counter_free 实现一个 8 位自由计数器，并说明它和 gray 计数器在跨时钟域读取时的差异。

lectures.title: 时钟域穿越：misc/sync
lectures.topic: 讲解 sync 子命名空间的 Bits/Reset/Pulse/Strobe/Vector 同步器，以及各自适用的信号类型与约束。
learning_goals: 区分 flag/reset/pulse/strobe/vector 同步器的适用场景
理解两级 D-FF 同步与 SYNC_DEPTH 参数
掌握 _meta/_async 信号的约束要求
lectures.practice_task: 为一个异步复位信号选择合适的 sync 核，并写出需要为 _meta/_async 信号添加的约束类型。

lectures.title: 外设与 IO：io 命名空间
lectures.topic: 讲解 io 命名空间下的 UART、DDR IO、IIC、消抖、七段显示、频率计数等外设控制器。
learning_goals: 了解 uart_tx/uart_rx 等 UART 收发结构
理解 ddrio_in/out/inout 的厂商专用 DDR 寄存器封装
掌握 io_Debounce 等慢速 IO 处理核
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 uart_tx.vhdl，列出它的 generic（如波特率分频）与端口，并画出一次起始位+数据位+停止位的发送时序。

units.title: 进阶：仿真、综合与目标平台

lectures.title: 仿真辅助包：sim 命名空间
lectures.topic: 讲解 src/sim 下的 sim_types/sim_global/sim_simulation/sim_random/sim_waveform 等仿真专用辅助包。
learning_goals: 掌握 simInitialize、simGenerateClock、simGenerateWaveform 等测试台入口
理解全局仿真状态与受保护类型的实现
了解随机数与波形生成的辅助接口
lectures.practice_task: 写一个最小测试台，调用 simInitialize 与 simGenerateClock 产生 100 MHz 时钟，并用 simGenerateWaveform 生成复位脉冲。

lectures.title: 测试台结构与编写
lectures.topic: 以 fifo_cc_got_tb 为例讲解 PoC 测试台的骨架、DUT 例化、generate 批量验证与板级 my_config 变体。
learning_goals: 掌握 _tb 实体 + tb 架构的标准写法
理解用 for generate 遍历多组 generic 的批量验证技巧
了解 tb/common 下各 my_config_<board> 变体的用途
lectures.practice_task: 仿照 fifo_cc_got_tb，为一个简单核（如 io_Debounce）搭建测试台骨架，包含时钟、复位生成与 DUT 例化。

lectures.title: VHDL 版本处理：v93 与 v08
lectures.topic: 讲解 PoC 如何用 .v93.vhdl / .v08.vhdl 后缀区分 VHDL 版本，以及受保护类型与 fileio 的版本差异实现。
learning_goals: 理解文件名后缀编码的 VHDL 版本约定
掌握 protected 类型在 v08 与 v93 的不同实现
了解 common.files 如何按版本条件选择文件
lectures.practice_task: 在 common.files 中找到依据 VHDLVersion 选择 fileio.v93/v08 的分支，并说明 protected.v08 为何只在 2008 版编译。

lectures.title: 上下文外综合与 netlist 流程
lectures.topic: 讲解 netlist/ 与 xst/ 目录如何支持把 PoC 核或厂商 IP 综合成网表，以及 .files 与模板配置文件的作用。
learning_goals: 理解 out-of-context 综合的目的
了解 xst 目录下按器件系列的 XST 配置
掌握 netlist/template.cgc 等模板的用途
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 xst/README.md 与 Series-7.xst，描述把一个 PoC 核综合成网表需要哪些配置输入。

lectures.title: 板级约束与 FPGA 目标
lectures.topic: 讲解 ucf/ 目录下各开发板（KC705、Atlys、ML505 等）的约束文件，以及 MetaStability 约束与板级 my_config 的配合。
learning_goals: 了解 ucf/xdc/sdc 约束文件的组织方式
理解 MetaStability.ucf 与同步器约束的关系
掌握板级 my_config 如何与约束文件配套
lectures.practice_task: 为 sync_Bits 核写出需要在 MetaStability.ucf 中添加的 _meta/_async 约束思路，并说明为何要 timing ignore 异步信号。

units.title: 专家：基础设施、子系统与二次开发

lectures.title: pyIPCMI 基础设施与命令行前端
lectures.topic: 讲解 poc.sh/poc.ps1 如何委托给 lib/pyIPCMI 这个 Python 子模块，统一驱动仿真、综合与 IP 生成流程。
learning_goals: 理解入口包装脚本到 pyIPCMI 的委托链
掌握 .files 文件被 pyIPCMI 消费的编译模型
了解 pyIPCMI 对多厂商工具链的抽象
lectures.practice_task: 追踪 poc.sh 的执行路径，描述从命令行参数到 lib/pyIPCMI 被加载的全过程，并解释 .files 在其中的角色。

lectures.title: 第三方验证库集成
lectures.topic: 讲解 lib/ 下 cocotb、OSVVM、UVVM、VUnit 四个子模块的定位，以及对应的 .files 集成清单。
learning_goals: 区分 cocotb/OSVVM/UVVM/VUnit 四种验证方法学
理解它们以 git submodule 形式集成的机制
掌握各 *.files 清单如何把第三方库接入编译
lectures.practice_task: 比较 OSVVM 与 UVVM 的定位，并说明在 .gitmodules 中它们各自指向哪个上游仓库。

lectures.title: cache 子系统深入
lectures.topic: 深入 cache 命名空间：cache_par/cache_cpu/cache_mem、标签单元与替换策略如何组成完整缓存。
learning_goals: 理解 cache_par 并行缓存的顶层结构
掌握 CPU 侧、存储侧与标签单元的分工
了解替换策略（如 LRU）的实现
lectures.practice_task: 在 cache_par.vhdl 中追踪一次命中读请求的数据通路，画出 cache_cpu→cache_tagunit→cache_mem 的交互时序。

lectures.title: 总线与流式协议
lectures.topic: 讲解 bus 命名空间的仲裁器与 stream（AXI-Stream 风格）数据流组件、Wishbone 适配。
learning_goals: 理解 bus_Arbiter 的仲裁机制
掌握 stream_Source/Sink/Mux/DeMux/Buffer 的流式接口
了解 Wishbone 总线适配器的接入方式
lectures.practice_task: 用 stream_Source 与 stream_Sink 搭建一个数据回环，并说明 valid/ready 握手协议的时序。

lectures.title: 网络协议栈：net 命名空间
lectures.topic: 讲解 net 下从 MAC、ARP、IPv4/IPv6 到 UDP、ICMP 的分层网络协议实现与 Wrapper 组装方式。
learning_goals: 理解 net 协议栈的分层与各 Wrapper 的封装职责
掌握 MAC 帧校验与 ARP 缓存机制
了解 IPv4/IPv6/UDP 收发通路的组成
lectures.practice_task: 梳理一个 UDP 包从 mac_Wrapper 进入到 udp_Wrapper 输出的逐层处理路径，并标注每层完成的工作。

lectures.title: 扩展 PoC：添加自己的 IP 核与命名空间
lectures.topic: 综合编码规范与命名空间模式，讲解如何为 PoC 贡献一个新核或新命名空间，包括文件、pkg、.files 与测试台。
learning_goals: 掌握新增一个 IP 核所需的完整文件清单
理解如何在命名空间 pkg 中声明新组件
了解配套测试台与 .files 更新的规范
minimal_modules: 测试台与 files 更新
lectures.practice_task: 在一个新命名空间 mylib 下创建一个简单核 mylib_echo，包含 entity/rtl 架构、pkg 组件声明与对应 _tb 测试台骨架。

data.head: 8c39b2407a97ec81b8601c4b64e4d2e2141ab9cf
data.rationale: PoC（Pile of Cores）是一个大型 VHDL/Verilog IP 核库，学习的关键不在于逐个记忆数百个核，而在于掌握贯穿全库的少数核心机制：命名空间分包模式、my_config/DEVICE_INFO 配置机制、基于 generate 的厂商可移植选择、公共包体系、仿真辅助包，以及 pyIPCMI 命令行基础设施。因此大纲分三层五单元：第 1 单元（入门）让读者从零认识项目定位、目录结构、运行配置与编码规范；第 2 单元（进阶）拆解 src/common 公共包与配置机制，这是理解一切后续核的基础；第 3 单元（进阶）讲解 IP 核的通用编写与组织模式，重点落在厂商选择、ocram/FIFO/arith/sync/io 等代表性家族；第 4 单元（进阶）覆盖仿真辅助包、测试台写法、VHDL 版本处理、综合流程与板级约束；第 5 单元（专家）深入 pyIPCMI 基础设施、第三方验证库集成，以及 cache/bus/net 复杂子系统，最后落到如何扩展 PoC。依赖关系呈清晰的有向无环图，每个讲义只解决一个主题并绑定真实源码与一个动手任务。

lectures.title: PoC 项目总览与定位
lectures.topic: 介绍 PoC（Pile of Cores）是什么、解决什么问题、由谁维护，以及它已迁移到 VHDL/PoC 仓库的现状。
learning_goals: 理解 PoC 是一个可复用的 VHDL/Verilog 硬件 IP 核库
知道 PoC 提供的核心能力（算术、缓存、CDC、FIFO、RAM、IO 控制器等）
了解项目的维护方与迁移到 VHDL/PoC 的现状
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README 的 Overview 部分，用自己的话写出 PoC 提供的三类硬件功能，并指出当前仓库与 VHDL/PoC 的关系。

lectures.title: 仓库目录结构解析
lectures.topic: 逐个解释 src、tb、lib、netlist、ucf、xst、py、sim、temp、tools 等顶层目录的用途，建立空间地图。
learning_goals: 记住 PoC 根目录的主要子目录及其职责
理解 src 按子命名空间分组、tb 镜像 src 的组织方式
区分源码、测试台、第三方库、约束、综合输出的位置
lectures.practice_task: 在本地克隆后画出 src/ 下各命名空间目录的树状图，并标注每个目录代表的功能类别（如 arith=算术、fifo=先入先出）。

lectures.title: 获取、运行与配置 PoC
lectures.topic: 讲解如何下载、用 poc.sh/poc.ps1 启动配置流程，以及 my_config.vhdl / my_project.vhdl 两个模板的作用。
learning_goals: 掌握 git clone --recursive 获取含子模块的 PoC
理解 poc.sh / poc.ps1 是调用 Python 基础设施的入口包装脚本
知道 my_config.vhdl 与 my_project.vhdl 的用途及如何从模板创建
lectures.practice_task: 从模板复制并填写 my_config.vhdl，把 MY_BOARD 设为 Custom、MY_DEVICE 设为 None，并说明这两个常量后续会被谁读取。

lectures.title: VHDL 编码规范与命名约定
lectures.topic: 通过 vhdl_coding.md 讲解 PoC 的命名、文件后缀、rtl/tb 架构名、文档头与代码风格约定。
learning_goals: 掌握 <namespace>_<entity> 的蛇形命名与单实体单文件约定
理解综合实现用 rtl 架构、测试台用 _tb 后缀和 tb 架构
了解文档头、缩进与大小写规范
lectures.practice_task: 参照 Entity.template 与 arith_addw.vhdl，为一个虚拟核 mylib_echo 写出符合规范的文件头、entity 声明与 rtl 架构骨架。

units.title: 进阶：公共包与配置机制

lectures.title: 公共包总览与 Common 上下文
lectures.topic: 介绍 src/common 下各公共包的职责，以及 common.vhdl 上下文如何一次性引用它们，并讲解 .files 编译顺序文件。
learning_goals: 了解 utils/config/math/strings/vectors/physical/components 等公共包的作用
理解 VHDL context Common 的批量引用机制
掌握 .files 文件如何描述带条件的编译顺序
lectures.practice_task: 在 common.files 中找到按条件包含 fileio 包的分支，解释为何不同 VHDL 版本会编译不同文件。

lectures.title: utils 包：通用类型与辅助函数
lectures.topic: 深入 utils.vhdl，讲解 T_BOOLVEC/T_INTVEC 等向量类型、整数子类型、枚举类型以及 ite/imin/imax/log2ceil 等常用函数。
learning_goals: 掌握 PoC 自定义的数组和子类型定义
学会使用 ite、imin、imax、log2ceil 等辅助函数
理解 SIMULATION 延迟常量的用途
lectures.practice_task: 在 utils.vhdl 中找到 log2ceil 的定义，写一段测试用例说明它如何把深度 30 映射到地址位宽 5。

lectures.title: 配置机制：my_config 与 config 包
lectures.topic: 讲解 my_config.vhdl 中的 MY_BOARD/MY_DEVICE 如何被 config.vhdl 解析，最终生成 DEVICE_INFO 与 VENDOR 枚举（VENDOR_ALTERA/XILINX/LATTICE 等）。
learning_goals: 理解 my_config 到 DEVICE_INFO 的解析链路
掌握 T_BOARD_INFO、T_DEVICE_INFO 与厂商枚举的作用
明白为什么后续核能根据厂商自动选择实现
lectures.practice_task: 把 MY_DEVICE 改成一个 Xilinx 型号，在 config.vhdl 中追踪它如何被识别为 VENDOR_XILINX，并描述 DEVICE_INFO 记录里包含哪些字段。

lectures.title: physical / strings / vectors / math 包
lectures.topic: 讲解物理类型包（如 FREQ，可在测试台写 100 MHz）、字符串处理、向量类型与数学扩展函数。
learning_goals: 掌握 FREQ/TIME 等物理类型及其字面量写法
了解字符串与向量（如 T_SLM）辅助函数
理解 math 包提供的扩展数学运算
lectures.practice_task: 利用 physical.vhdl 的 FREQ 类型，写出表示 50 MHz 与 10 ns 的常量，并计算二者一个周期的关系。

lectures.title: components 包：用函数描述原语
lectures.topic: 讲解 components.vhdl 如何把触发器（ffdre/ffse）、多路选择器等常用原语以函数形式提供，便于一行调用。
learning_goals: 理解把触发器写成函数的设计思路
掌握 ffdre/ffse/rs 触发器等函数的参数
了解 INIT 参数表示复位值而非上电初值的注意事项
lectures.practice_task: 用 components.vhdl 中的 ffdre 函数实现一个带使能与复位的单比特寄存器进程，并说明 INIT 的真实含义。

units.title: 进阶：IP 核模式与命名空间

lectures.title: 命名空间包模式
lectures.topic: 讲解每个命名空间的 <ns>.pkg.vhdl 如何集中声明组件、类型与函数（以 arith.pkg、fifo.pkg、mem.pkg 为例）。
learning_goals: 掌握命名空间包作为组件声明清单的作用
理解 generic/port 在包中集中声明的复用价值
能够阅读一个新命名空间的 pkg 文件快速了解其核
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 fifo.pkg.vhdl，列出它声明的全部 FIFO 组件名，并标注每个组件的使用场景（如 cc=同钟、ic=跨钟）。

lectures.title: 厂商选择与可移植机制
lectures.topic: 讲解 PoC 如何用 generate 语句依据 DEVICE_INFO.Vendor 在通用实现与厂商专用实现（如 sync_Bits_Xilinx / _Altera）间选择。
learning_goals: 理解可移植实体 + 厂商专用子实体的分层结构
掌握 if generate 选择 VENDOR_ALTERA/XILINX 的写法
了解 ASYNC_REG/SHREG_EXTRACT 等综合属性的作用
lectures.practice_task: 在 sync_Bits.vhdl 中定位 vendor 选择逻辑，画出让一个 Generic 设备走入哪条分支的流程图。

lectures.title: 片上 RAM 抽象：ocram 家族
lectures.topic: 讲解 ocram（on-chip RAM）命名空间如何抽象单端口、简单双端口、真双端口等存储器并对接厂商原语。
learning_goals: 区分 ocram_sp/sdp/tdp/esdp/wf 等存储器配置
理解 ocram 如何根据厂商实例化 altera 版本
掌握读优先/写优先等存储行为差异
lectures.practice_task: 比较 ocram_sp.vhdl 的通用实现与 ocram_sp_altera.vhdl，说明 Altera 分支调用了哪个底层原语。

learning_goals: 区分 cc（同钟）与 ic（独立钟）FIFO
理解 put/got/valid/full 流水线接口语义
掌握 DATA_REG/OUTPUT_REG/STATE_BITS 等关键 generic
lectures.practice_task: 实例化一个 fifo_cc_got，配置 D_BITS=8、MIN_DEPTH=30，并解释 estate_wr/fstate_rd 填充指示器的含义。

lectures.topic: 讲解 arith 命名空间下的计数器、宽加法、伪随机数、优先编码、开方与除法等算术核。
learning_goals: 掌握 free/gray/bcd/ring 等各类计数器
理解 arith_firstone 优先编码与令牌传递
了解 arith_prng 伪随机与 arith_sqrt 除法实现
lectures.practice_task: 实例化 arith_counter_free 实现一个 8 位自由计数器，并说明它和 gray 计数器在跨时钟域读取时的差异。

lectures.title: 时钟域穿越：misc/sync
lectures.topic: 讲解 sync 子命名空间的 Bits/Reset/Pulse/Strobe/Vector 同步器，以及各自适用的信号类型与约束。

理解两级 D-FF 同步与 SYNC_DEPTH 参数
掌握 _meta/_async 信号的约束要求
lectures.practice_task: 为一个异步复位信号选择合适的 sync 核，并写出需要为 _meta/_async 信号添加的约束类型。

lectures.title: 外设与 IO：io 命名空间
lectures.topic: 讲解 io 命名空间下的 UART、DDR IO、IIC、消抖、七段显示、频率计数等外设控制器。
learning_goals: 了解 uart_tx/uart_rx 等 UART 收发结构
理解 ddrio_in/out/inout 的厂商专用 DDR 寄存器封装
掌握 io_Debounce 等慢速 IO 处理核
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 uart_tx.vhdl，列出它的 generic（如波特率分频）与端口，并画出一次起始位+数据位+停止位的发送时序。

units.title: 进阶：仿真、综合与目标平台

lectures.title: 仿真辅助包：sim 命名空间
lectures.topic: 讲解 src/sim 下的 sim_types/sim_global/sim_simulation/sim_random/sim_waveform 等仿真专用辅助包。
learning_goals: 掌握 simInitialize、simGenerateClock、simGenerateWaveform 等测试台入口
理解全局仿真状态与受保护类型的实现
了解随机数与波形生成的辅助接口
lectures.practice_task: 写一个最小测试台，调用 simInitialize 与 simGenerateClock 产生 100 MHz 时钟，并用 simGenerateWaveform 生成复位脉冲。

lectures.title: 测试台结构与编写
lectures.topic: 以 fifo_cc_got_tb 为例讲解 PoC 测试台的骨架、DUT 例化、generate 批量验证与板级 my_config 变体。
learning_goals: 掌握 _tb 实体 + tb 架构的标准写法
理解用 for generate 遍历多组 generic 的批量验证技巧
了解 tb/common 下各 my_config_<board> 变体的用途
lectures.practice_task: 仿照 fifo_cc_got_tb，为一个简单核（如 io_Debounce）搭建测试台骨架，包含时钟、复位生成与 DUT 例化。

lectures.title: VHDL 版本处理：v93 与 v08
lectures.topic: 讲解 PoC 如何用 .v93.vhdl / .v08.vhdl 后缀区分 VHDL 版本，以及受保护类型与 fileio 的版本差异实现。
learning_goals: 理解文件名后缀编码的 VHDL 版本约定
掌握 protected 类型在 v08 与 v93 的不同实现
了解 common.files 如何按版本条件选择文件
lectures.practice_task: 在 common.files 中找到依据 VHDLVersion 选择 fileio.v93/v08 的分支，并说明 protected.v08 为何只在 2008 版编译。

lectures.title: 上下文外综合与 netlist 流程
lectures.topic: 讲解 netlist/ 与 xst/ 目录如何支持把 PoC 核或厂商 IP 综合成网表，以及 .files 与模板配置文件的作用。
learning_goals: 理解 out-of-context 综合的目的
了解 xst 目录下按器件系列的 XST 配置
掌握 netlist/template.cgc 等模板的用途
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 xst/README.md 与 Series-7.xst，描述把一个 PoC 核综合成网表需要哪些配置输入。

lectures.title: 板级约束与 FPGA 目标
lectures.topic: 讲解 ucf/ 目录下各开发板（KC705、Atlys、ML505 等）的约束文件，以及 MetaStability 约束与板级 my_config 的配合。
learning_goals: 了解 ucf/xdc/sdc 约束文件的组织方式
理解 MetaStability.ucf 与同步器约束的关系
掌握板级 my_config 如何与约束文件配套
lectures.practice_task: 为 sync_Bits 核写出需要在 MetaStability.ucf 中添加的 _meta/_async 约束思路，并说明为何要 timing ignore 异步信号。

units.title: 专家：基础设施、子系统与二次开发

lectures.title: pyIPCMI 基础设施与命令行前端
lectures.topic: 讲解 poc.sh/poc.ps1 如何委托给 lib/pyIPCMI 这个 Python 子模块，统一驱动仿真、综合与 IP 生成流程。
learning_goals: 理解入口包装脚本到 pyIPCMI 的委托链
掌握 .files 文件被 pyIPCMI 消费的编译模型
了解 pyIPCMI 对多厂商工具链的抽象
lectures.practice_task: 追踪 poc.sh 的执行路径，描述从命令行参数到 lib/pyIPCMI 被加载的全过程，并解释 .files 在其中的角色。

lectures.title: 第三方验证库集成
lectures.topic: 讲解 lib/ 下 cocotb、OSVVM、UVVM、VUnit 四个子模块的定位，以及对应的 .files 集成清单。
learning_goals: 区分 cocotb/OSVVM/UVVM/VUnit 四种验证方法学
理解它们以 git submodule 形式集成的机制
掌握各 *.files 清单如何把第三方库接入编译
lectures.practice_task: 比较 OSVVM 与 UVVM 的定位，并说明在 .gitmodules 中它们各自指向哪个上游仓库。

lectures.title: cache 子系统深入
lectures.topic: 深入 cache 命名空间：cache_par/cache_cpu/cache_mem、标签单元与替换策略如何组成完整缓存。
learning_goals: 理解 cache_par 并行缓存的顶层结构
掌握 CPU 侧、存储侧与标签单元的分工
了解替换策略（如 LRU）的实现
lectures.practice_task: 在 cache_par.vhdl 中追踪一次命中读请求的数据通路，画出 cache_cpu→cache_tagunit→cache_mem 的交互时序。

lectures.title: 总线与流式协议
lectures.topic: 讲解 bus 命名空间的仲裁器与 stream（AXI-Stream 风格）数据流组件、Wishbone 适配。
learning_goals: 理解 bus_Arbiter 的仲裁机制
掌握 stream_Source/Sink/Mux/DeMux/Buffer 的流式接口
了解 Wishbone 总线适配器的接入方式
lectures.practice_task: 用 stream_Source 与 stream_Sink 搭建一个数据回环，并说明 valid/ready 握手协议的时序。

lectures.title: 网络协议栈：net 命名空间
lectures.topic: 讲解 net 下从 MAC、ARP、IPv4/IPv6 到 UDP、ICMP 的分层网络协议实现与 Wrapper 组装方式。
learning_goals: 理解 net 协议栈的分层与各 Wrapper 的封装职责
掌握 MAC 帧校验与 ARP 缓存机制
了解 IPv4/IPv6/UDP 收发通路的组成
lectures.practice_task: 梳理一个 UDP 包从 mac_Wrapper 进入到 udp_Wrapper 输出的逐层处理路径，并标注每层完成的工作。

lectures.title: 扩展 PoC：添加自己的 IP 核与命名空间
lectures.topic: 综合编码规范与命名空间模式，讲解如何为 PoC 贡献一个新核或新命名空间，包括文件、pkg、.files 与测试台。
learning_goals: 掌握新增一个 IP 核所需的完整文件清单
理解如何在命名空间 pkg 中声明新组件
了解配套测试台与 .files 更新的规范
minimal_modules: 测试台与 files 更新
lectures.practice_task: 在一个新命名空间 mylib 下创建一个简单核 mylib_echo，包含 entity/rtl 架构、pkg 组件声明与对应 _tb 测试台骨架。
